name: Boeing 747-8
details: The Boeing 747-8 is the latest and largest variant of the iconic 747 family, often called the 'Queen of the Skies.' It features a stretched fuselage, redesigned wings, and advanced aerodynamics. Despite the rise of twin-engine wide-bodies, the 747-8 remains beloved for its grace and capacity.

name: Airbus A350-900
details: The Airbus A350-900 is a next-generation long-range wide-body airliner built with 53% composite materials. Its revolutionary design delivers 25% lower fuel burn and a remarkably quiet cabin. The A350 has become the flagship of many world-class airlines.

name: Cessna Citation Longitude
details: The Cessna Citation Longitude is a super-midsize business jet offering the largest cabin in its class. With full fly-by-wire controls and advanced avionics, it provides a smooth, quiet ride. The stand-up cabin features a flat floor and configurable seating.

name: Lockheed Martin F-35 Lightning II
details: The F-35 Lightning II is the world's most advanced multirole fighter aircraft. With stealth technology, sensor fusion, and network-enabled capabilities, it dominates in air-to-air and air-to-ground missions. Three variants serve the Air Force, Navy, and Marine Corps.

name: Boeing 777F
details: The Boeing 777 Freighter is the world's longest-range twin-engine freighter. With the lowest operating cost per tonne-mile in its class, it has become the backbone of global air cargo operations. Its large cargo door and powered cargo handling system enable rapid loading.

name: Gulfstream G700
details: The Gulfstream G700 is the ultimate ultra-long-range business jet, offering the tallest, widest, and longest cabin in its class. With revolutionary wing design and Rolls-Royce Pearl 700 engines, it delivers unmatched range and speed for intercontinental travel.

name: Airbus A320neo
details: The Airbus A320neo (New Engine Option) is the workhorse of short-to-medium-haul aviation. With 15-20% better fuel efficiency than previous models, it has become the best-selling single-aisle aircraft family. Advanced sharklets and cabin improvements enhance the passenger experience.

name: Boeing C-17 Globemaster III
details: The C-17 Globemaster III is the backbone of military strategic airlift. It can carry outsized cargo including tanks, helicopters, and troops directly to austere forward-operating locations. Its ability to land on short, unpaved runways makes it indispensable for global military operations.

name: Pilatus PC-24
details: The Pilatus PC-24 is the world's first 'super versatile jet' — designed to operate from short, unpaved, and grass airstrips that other jets cannot access. Its rugged landing gear and trailing-link design open up a world of destinations unreachable by conventional business jets.

name: Antonov An-124
details: The Antonov An-124 Ruslan is one of the largest cargo aircraft ever built. Designed for transporting heavy and oversized loads, it has carried everything from satellites to wind turbine blades. Its nose cargo door and kneeling landing gear enable drive-on/drive-off loading.

name: Dassault Rafale
details: The Dassault Rafale is France's premier omnirole combat aircraft, capable of performing air superiority, ground attack, reconnaissance, and nuclear deterrence missions. Its delta-canard design provides exceptional agility, and its SPECTRA electronic warfare system makes it virtually undetectable.

name: Embraer E195-E2
details: The Embraer E195-E2 is the largest member of Embraer's E2 family of next-generation regional jets. Its new high-aspect-ratio wing and Pratt & Whitney GTF engines deliver 25% better fuel efficiency. The 2-2 seating configuration means no middle seats for most passengers.
